skill.name: promote-skill
skill.description: Guides agents through preparing, publishing, and promoting a public SKILL.md file across skill marketplaces and GitHub-based distribution paths.
skill.summary: Prepare, publish, and promote public agent skills across marketplaces.
skill.author: Hermes Agent

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell-command, network, filesystem, and weak-crypto patterns, but review shows this file is documentation for publishing skills, not executable code. The weak-crypto and path-traversal hits are false positives from text such as SKILL.md, placeholder URLs, and ellipses. Remaining risk is medium because the skill instructs agents to run marketplace CLIs, GitHub commands, copy files into /tmp, and publish public source URLs, which can expose data if used without review.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Guided External Publishing Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill provides shell examples for marketplace CLIs, GitHub gists, Git repositories, and publishing commands. These are legitimate for the stated purpose, but they can create public resources or modify repositories if executed without confirming authentication, target owner, and dry-run status.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly visible in fenced examples and match the skill purpose. They are not hidden execution, but they can perform external side effects when followed by an agent.

medium_findings.title: Public Source Publishing Can Expose Sensitive Skill Content
medium_findings.description: The workflow asks the user to create raw public SKILL.md URLs and publish listings across marketplaces. The file includes warnings to scan secrets, but the operation still carries data exposure risk if private instructions, tokens, or paid artifacts are uploaded publicly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The public-publishing workflow is explicit and the skill itself acknowledges credential and paywall risks. This is a real operational risk, not evidence of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Many external-command alerts come from Markdown inline code such as SKILL.md, command names, route placeholders, and marketplace examples. These are not Ruby backtick execution because the skill is a Markdown document, not a Ruby program.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows Markdown formatting and command documentation, not executable Ruby source. Some documented commands remain operationally sensitive, so they are covered separately as medium risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Marketplace URLs Are Benign Documentation
low_findings.description: The detected URLs are placeholder raw source URLs or public marketplace examples. No evidence found of hidden exfiltration, tracking endpoints, or automatic network requests inside executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in documentation examples and final report templates. They are still relevant because agents may use them to create public listings.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Findings Are Scoped Examples
low_findings.description: The /tmp directory and copy commands are used in a documented repo-creation example. The /dev/null usage suppresses grep errors during a secrets scan. The path traversal alerts at line 177 are false positives from ellipses in a gist URL example, not filesystem traversal.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines show ordinary documentation for grep, temporary workspace commands, and URL placeholder text. No evidence found of arbitrary file reads or traversal.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts occur on lines containing SKILL.md and related publishing text, not cryptographic code. The reconnaissance alerts point to anonymous-browser verification and a Kilo install command, not system probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows text, placeholders, and publishing instructions. No evidence found of weak crypto primitives or host reconnaissance commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands With External Side Effects
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples include commands that publish marketplace listings, create public gists, initialize Git repositories, and push GitHub repos. Agents should require user confirmation before live execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are real examples and include operations that can publish data. The surrounding text recommends dry runs and verification, which reduces but does not remove risk.

content.value_statement: Publishing a skill across marketplaces requires safe packaging, public source links, and platform-specific steps. This skill gives agents a repeatable checklist for validation, publication, and follow-up promotion.
actual_capabilities: Collects required listing details such as title, slug, description, category, tags, and price.
Defines a pre-publication secrets scan and manual review checklist for SKILL.md files.
Provides uGig CLI and browser workflow guidance for public skill listings.
Documents sh1pt commands for dry-run marketplace promotion manifests.
Compares marketplace submission paths for uGig, ClawHub, Goose, Kilo, Skillstore, and related platforms.
Provides a final report format for published links and manual next steps.
limitations: It does not perform marketplace submission by itself unless the agent runs external tools.
It depends on valid user accounts, installed CLIs, and marketplace availability.
It cannot guarantee that every marketplace accepts the submitted skill.
It cannot prove a public artifact is paywalled without external verification.

use_cases.title: Release a New Agent Skill
use_cases.description: Prepare a local SKILL.md, scan it for secrets, create a public source URL, and publish an initial marketplace listing.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Multi-Marketplace Promotion
use_cases.description: Build a checklist for uGig, sh1pt, GitHub, ClawHub, Goose, Kilo, and manual submission channels.

use_cases.title: Audit a Skill Before Public Upload
use_cases.description: Review credentials, public visibility, price expectations, and listing metadata before sharing the skill file.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Listing
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for one marketplace listing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use promote-skill to prepare my local SKILL.md for a public marketplace listing. Ask for any missing title, category, tags, source URL, and price.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Safe Preflight
prompt_templates.prompt: Use promote-skill to review this skill for public release. Focus on secrets, private URLs, paid-content exposure, and metadata gaps before any upload.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Promotion Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use promote-skill to create a marketplace promotion plan for uGig, sh1pt, GitHub, Goose, Kilo, Skillstore, and manual channels. Separate automated, dry-run, and manual steps.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Publication Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use promote-skill to produce a final publication report with published URLs, raw source links, pending marketplace actions, scan status, and verification notes.

output_examples.input: Prepare my SKILL.md for uGig and GitHub publication.
output_examples.output: A publication checklist with required metadata, secrets review status, recommended source URL type, uGig listing fields, and GitHub repository steps.

output_examples.input: Plan promotion for a skill that should be free on every marketplace.
output_examples.output: A marketplace matrix showing free listing settings, raw SKILL.md requirements, CLI options, manual submission steps, and verification tasks.

output_examples.input: Summarize what was published and what still needs manual work.
output_examples.output: A concise final report with published links, raw source links, ready/manual next steps, and security scan notes.

best_practices: Run a secrets scan and manually inspect every hit before creating a public source URL.
Use dry-run commands until authentication, account targets, and repository ownership are verified.
Prefer a public GitHub repository when marketplaces require repository metadata or future updates.
anti_patterns: Do not publish credentials, cookies, private environment values, or internal URLs in SKILL.md.
Do not assume a public raw URL is paywalled without anonymous-browser verification.
Do not run unknown marketplace CLIs live before checking installation, login state, and target account.

faq.question: Does this skill publish directly to every marketplace?
faq.answer: No. It provides commands and checklist steps. Many marketplaces require accounts, API keys, pull requests, or manual review.

faq.question: Can I use a GitHub gist as the source URL?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports a public gist raw URL, but a repository is better for auto-indexed marketplaces.

faq.question: What should I check before uploading a skill?
faq.answer: Check for secrets, private URLs, paid content exposure, incomplete metadata, and marketplace-specific account requirements.

faq.question: Why does the skill mention dry-run commands?
faq.answer: Dry runs help verify generated commands before any public listing, repository, or marketplace submission is created.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for paid skills?
faq.answer: Use caution. Public raw URLs may expose the artifact, so verify paywall behavior from an anonymous browser.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code in this marketplace report.
